bull.label: Upside
bull.whatHappens: By year three, at least one urban robotaxi network clears internal unit-economic hurdles; additional metros widen driverless domains without rolling back prior approvals; personal-auto loss costs show durable frequency improvement in disclosed data.
bull.whatConfirms: Sequential permit expansion, rising autonomous passenger-mile share without sustained safety regression, and carrier commentary that ties frequency to pool composition—not only rate.
bull.positionImpact: Long compute and stack exposure works; personal-lines hedges need tight timing discipline. Legacy OEM longs without fleet leverage lag.

base.label: Base
base.whatHappens: Deployment advances in steps with pauses after incidents; liability and insurance terms adjust slowly; ownership drifts down in dense cities while suburbs move later.
base.whatConfirms: Regulatory advance-and-pause pattern, mixed carrier results, residual pressure concentrated in urban segments.
base.positionImpact: Thesis remains operative but path-dependent; size favors liquid enablers over concentrated single-name operator risk. Shorts sized for earnings noise.

bear.label: Downside
bear.whatHappens: Safety or political backlash narrows domains for an extended period; carriers retrench without clear frequency data; OEMs defer city autonomy and reallocate to driver-assist.
bear.whatConfirms: Permit pullbacks, severity-led narratives without offsetting frequency evidence, and capex cuts to urban L4 programs.
bear.positionImpact: Narrow autonomy longs underperform; personal-auto writers may stabilize; diversified platforms with non-AV earnings outperform single-theme names.
